cross_asset_summaries.credit: 信用市場廣泛參與漲勢，但融資透明度與公司治理仍使發行人表現分化。
cross_asset_summaries.dollar_commodities: 隨著避險需求消退，美元走弱與油價下跌緩解了通膨及新興市場融資壓力。

header.stance: 油價帶動的美債舒緩行情擴及實質殖利率，但限制性利率風險猶存

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開篇主論點

6月11日收盤逆轉了前一交易日的熊市趨陡走勢，但並未化解其下的限制性政策爭論。美債從2年期券一路上漲至長債，10年期殖利率收於4.45%，下跌10個基點。這與6月10日形成明確轉折；當時核心消費者通膨降溫，卻未能拉低殖利率。新的催化劑來自地緣政治：市場重新相信美國與伊朗的衝突可能朝協議邁進，推低原油，並消除名目殖利率及市場預期聯準會政策路徑中所包含的部分能源溢價。

最強的傳導路徑由局勢降溫的消息，依序經過油價、通膨補償及利率預期。短期利率合約將市場完全定價的25個基點升息，從12月延後至2027年第1季。實質殖利率也告下滑，顯示這波漲勢不只是損益兩平通膨率機械式下降所致。能源風險降低，減輕了前瞻政策制約、改善成長與通膨組合，並在股市大漲之際鼓勵存續期間需求。因此，這是由油價帶動、針對衝擊分布的重新定價，而非因成長疑慮引發的防禦性避險。

國內數據構成主要反向力量。5月最終需求生產者物價較4月上升1.1%，能源推高商品成本，基礎指標也高於預期。然而，首次與續領失業救濟人數均上升，使通膨高於預期未能成為毫無保留的緊縮信號。這種組合讓Fed面臨棘手的不對稱局面：上游通膨仍過強，不足以驗證寬鬆，但勞動市場指標轉弱與能源風險溢價下降，則降低了升息的急迫性。

供給與全球政策使這波漲勢無法乾淨俐落地回歸低利率假設。長債增額發行在投標倍數改善且直接投標踴躍之下獲得消化，但長端表現仍略遜於中段。由於能源通膨正在擴散，ECB升息，官員也保留近期再次升息的可能。信用市場強化了相同的條件式訊息：高評級銀行與大型AI借款人取得巨額融資，但公司治理有缺陷的發行案，即使提供高殖利率仍告失敗。主導解讀由通膨型趨陡轉為廣泛的地緣政治舒緩，但偏高的實質殖利率、具選擇性的信用融資管道及海外緊縮，仍使市場難以斷言限制性格局已經化解。

## 美債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美債全面上漲，中段表現略優。2年期殖利率下跌8個基點至4.05%，5年期下跌9個基點至4.18%，10年期下跌10個基點至4.45%。30年期殖利率收低8個基點至4.95%。此波走勢使長債殖利率重返5%以下，並完全收復6月10日的跌幅，但政策敏感型殖利率仍遠高於聯邦資金利率目標區間。這次收盤重新定價了可能採取限制措施的時點，而非引入寬鬆路徑。

殖利率曲線變化有序。2s10s斜率收窄2個基點至40，而由於2年期與30年期殖利率等幅下跌，2s30s維持在90。2s5s10s蝶式利差維持-14不變，顯示5年期券的相對曲線形態穩定。隨10年期券領漲，2s10s30s蝶式利差下降4個基點至-10。這種溫和的牛市趨平與前一日的熊市趨陡形成對比，但並未造成新的中段錯位或長端單獨失守。

在10年期券方面，實質殖利率與通膨補償各自貢獻名目殖利率跌幅的一半。10年期TIPS殖利率下跌5個基點至2.16%，10年期損益兩平通膨率也下降5個基點至2.29%。5年期實質殖利率同樣下跌5個基點至1.78%；7年期下跌6個基點至1.96%，20年期與30年期實質殖利率則分別下降5及6個基點至2.53%及2.72%。損益兩平通膨率下降反映油價傳導，而實質殖利率同步回落，則反映市場認為近期緊縮政策的必要性下降，且對不利成長與通膨組合所要求的補償減少。

拉長比較期間可使這波舒緩行情更具脈絡。2年期殖利率1週不變，且較1個月前仍高10個基點。5年期殖利率1週同樣持平，較1個月前高11個基點。10年期較1週前低2個基點，但較1個月前仍高3個基點；30年期則較1週前低2個基點，較1個月前低3個基點。累積重新定價仍集中在前端與中段，因政策時點及預期中性利率對這些區段影響最大。

5月生產者物價對漲勢的持久性構成挑戰。最終需求月增1.1%、年增6.5%，高於公開行事曆預估的月增0.7%。扣除食品、能源及貿易服務的最終需求月增0.8%、年增5.1%，也高於月增0.5%的預估。商品價格大漲2.8%，其中能源上漲10.7%居首，服務價格則上漲0.3%。漲勢並非只限於整體能源，使市場無法將數據視為單一項目衝擊而置之不理。

勞動市場數據則指向相反方向。截至6月6日當週，首次申領失業救濟人數增加4,000人至229,000人，高於220,000人的預估，4週移動平均則升至219,000人。截至5月30日當週，續領失業救濟人數增加24,000人至179.5萬人。投保失業率維持在1.2%，因此這份數據並未顯示勞動市場突然崩解。但數據確實顯示，開始領取及持續領取救濟的勞工均增加，及時制衡了批發通膨高於預期的影響。

短期利率合約透過延後、而非刪除緊縮風險，來消化這項矛盾。在地緣政治消息出現前，市場已完全定價12月前升息25個基點。到收盤時，這個時點已延後至2027年第1季。此反應與實質殖利率及損益兩平通膨率同步下降相符：油價下跌降低了價格持續轉嫁的風險，而申領失業救濟人數走強則降低了等待的代價。生產者物價漲勢廣泛，仍使6月16-17日的FOMC會議無法對通膨發出簡單的警報解除訊號。

$220億的30年期增額發行以5.020%得標，投標倍數為2.33，高於前次規模較大標售的2.30。間接投標人獲配59.95%，直接投標人獲配25.31%，交易商獲配14.74%。這批票面利率5%的債券於6月15日交割，並於2056年5月到期。由於未保留發行前交易基準，需求訊號須由投標倍數及分配結構界定。投標倍數改善、直接投標占比異常偏高及交易商餘額有限，顯示終端投資人具承接能力；不過相較5月，本次發行規模較小，也降低了資產負債表負擔。

國庫券標售同樣顯示需求有序但有所分化。美國財政部接受$700億的4週期國庫券投標，最高利率為3.595%，投標倍數3.13，高於前次的3.07；間接投標人獲配70.59%。$750.01億的8週期國庫券以3.610%得標，投標倍數由3.20降至2.94，間接投標占比為69.59%。兩者均於6月16日交割。規模縮減的較短天期標售表現改善，而供給不變的8週期國庫券投標倍數轉弱。2項結果皆未伴隨超短期現金殖利率普遍承壓。

以附息債券等值計算的國庫券殖利率收於：4週期3.67%、6週期3.68%，兩者均持平。8週期殖利率上升1個基點至3.69%，13週期、17週期及26週期殖利率則各下降1個基點至3.71%、3.76%及3.80%。52週期殖利率下降5個基點至3.85%，與短期利率合約中升息延後的調整相符。前端仍呈正斜率，但政策溢價已從最近期區段往後移動。

準備金管理降低了下一波國庫券供給增加演變為突發融資衝擊的風險。Fed維持截至7月13日的每月期間內，規劃購買約$100億的美國財政部 bills，並安排約$165億的再投資購買。截至6月10日當週，銀行準備金增加$658億至$3.111兆。這些操作旨在維持充裕準備金，因美國財政部最終會將現金餘額重建至$1兆以上；若無相關操作，這一過程將從銀行體系抽走流動性。

因此，利率收盤同時傳達2項訊息。地緣政治舒緩的力量足以壓過熾熱的PPI數據，並拉低實質殖利率與通膨補償。標售參與及準備金管理購債，使供給未能中斷這波走勢。然而，生產者物價漲勢廣泛、國庫券殖利率曲線向上傾斜及實質殖利率仍高，使漲勢無法轉化為寬鬆敘事。下一步政策行動看來是延後，而不一定是下調。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

信用市場參與廣泛改善，上市總報酬指標與美債及股市同步上漲。LQD上漲0.8506%，HYG上漲0.5914%，JNK上漲0.5745%。投資等級債漲幅較大，主要受政府債券殖利率下降帶來的存續期間貢獻，而高收益債走揚則顯示，本交易日的表現並非依賴企業風險偏好惡化。各評級的初級市場均保持開放，但發行人結構及融資清晰度造成截然不同的結果。

Citibank以1筆橫跨2至6年的$62.5億、4部分發行案，展現高評級債券的強勁承接。最長天期券的價格指引由最初約高於美債95個基點，收緊至67個基點。這些營運銀行債券預計分別獲Moody’s、S&P及Fitch給予Aa3、A+及AA-評級，募資用途為一般企業用途。資本規則調整降低了Citi的發債急迫性，使其發行量低於同業，因此這筆交易增加供給，卻不像是陷入困境的資產負債表修復。

這筆銀行交易身處活躍的投資等級債發行環境。華爾街另外5大銀行年內已售出超過$1,200億，包括第1季創紀錄、超過$650億的發行量。Citi是本交易日3家發行超過$100億美國高評級債券的借款人之一。價格較初始指引收緊，顯示即使總體市場波動，投資人仍可消化這個較晚進場的發行人；而短至中天期的到期結構，也避免將全部負擔加諸長存續期間投資組合。

Oracle則針對融資透明度，提供了更明確的現券信用反應。其2041年到期、票息3.65%的債券，截至午盤收緊15個基點至2個百分點的利差；2052年到期、票息6.9%的債券則收窄12個基點至2.09個百分點。5年期違約保障成本也告下降。此前Oracle表示，預計本曆年不會再次發債，並將以規劃中的$400億債務與股權組合，為本會計年度提供資金，其中包括先前宣布、透過市價發行方式籌集的$200億股票資金。

這份舒緩並未消除Oracle龐大的資本需求。會計年度資本支出達$557億，高於公司的$500億預測，新年度淨資本支出預計接近$700億。客戶預付款及客戶提供的運算設備可降低Oracle近期的現金負擔，但該公司仍是美國高評級指數中最大的非金融發行人，債券規模約$1,170億。即使股市質疑支出本身的回報，信用投資人仍對範圍明確的融資計畫給予正面回應。

CoreWeave將AI融資拓展至風險更高且更全球化的管道。這家垃圾級雲端公司發行€20億、6年期歐元債券，殖利率為8.5%；另發行$12.5億、6年期美元債券，殖利率為9.625%。歐元債券訂單超過€70億，吸引大多數歐洲主要高收益債買家。Ba3、B+及BB-的評級使該交易品質遠低於超大規模雲端服務商，而預估約$350億的投資及約$260億的現金消耗，則使再融資與執行風險維持高檔。訂單簿顯示其具有融資管道；高殖利率則為槓桿與獲利能力不確定性保留了龐大溢價。

Hanuman Wind Power則標示出融資管道的界線。Energy Absolute旗下這家公司在將收單期延長1天後，第2度延後其國際垃圾債券第1次發行。約9%中段至高段的殖利率，仍無法克服市場對其泰國母公司治理、過往詐欺指控，以及部分募資款擬轉交母公司的疑慮。15年期美債殖利率上升，也削弱擬議中15年期、5年後可贖回結構的相對估值。投資人願意以一定價格接受積極的AI擴張，卻拒絕治理與現金流範圍仍不清楚的結構。

因此，初級市場承接強勁，卻未達到來者不拒的程度。高評級銀行債利差較初始指引大幅收緊，Oracle在提出設有上限的融資時程後，其流通債券上漲，而CoreWeave則透過獲超額認購的歐元券，完成跨幣別融資多元化。Hanuman一再延後發行，顯示單靠殖利率無法修復薄弱治理。美債殖利率下降改善了機械性的融資環境，但資訊揭露、擔保順位、買方基礎的廣度，以及對未來資本需求的信心，決定各借款人能獲得多少好處。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

ECB的行動方向與美國地緣政治重新定價相反。由於能源衝擊正在擴散至間接成本與通膨預期，ECB將存款利率上調25個基點至2.25%，為近3年來第1次升息。總裁Christine Lagarde表示，經濟活動正在放緩，尤其是服務業，但她認為經濟足以承受更緊縮的政策。這項決策使歐元區成為第1個以升息回應戰爭驅動之價格衝擊的主要經濟體。

儘管ECB採取行動，歐洲存續期間資產仍然上漲。決策公布後，10年期殖利率下跌3個基點至3.05%，歐元則維持在$1.1538附近。這項反應顯示升息已獲充分預期，而其成長代價限制了市場無限延伸升息循環的意願。ECB預測仍顯示通膨到2027年上半年都將高於目標，使官員擔心，單憑和平協議可能無法逆轉已透過薪資、服務及預期傳導的第2輪效應。

後續風險仍具實質意義。官員並未排除最快7月再次升息的可能，儘管9月仍是較普遍的預期；投資人則定價在新的2.25%利率之上，還會有2次額外行動。IMF假設2026年累計緊縮50個基點，並將歐元區通膨預測由2.4%上調至2.8%。IMF也將成長展望由1.1%下調至0.9%，界定了央行的限制：通膨擴散支持採取限制性政策，但需求轉弱最終可能減少所需的限制程度。

挪威顯示，相同的能源衝擊未必導致同步行動。挪威央行1項調查預測，第2季產出成長0.2%、第3季成長0.3%，低於先前對第2季成長0.4%的看法。招募困難與產能限制緩解，儘管核心通膨最近加速，仍降低6月再次升息的壓力。薪資成長預期升至4.5%，且石油帶來的意外收益支撐挪威克朗，因此維持利率不變的理由，是經濟活動轉弱與貨幣走強，而非不存在價格壓力。

在6月18日Makerfield補選與英格蘭銀行決策前，英國財政不確定性增添了另一層長存續期間溢價。傳統英國公債總額約£2.1兆，若加上指數連結債與國庫券，公共債務證券規模約為£2.9兆。國債預計於2027年達GDP的105.4%，高於2023年的98.8%。由於潛在的Labour黨魁挑戰可能改變借款計畫，投資人仍對長端保持警惕；4月預算赤字也已達該月份6年來最高水準。

這些發展對美債存續期間形成方向相反的拉力。ECB預期中的後續行動與英國未定的財政政策，提高了其他主權債券殖利率曲線所提供的報酬，可能促使全球買家對長天期美債要求更高回報。挪威經濟活動轉弱與歐洲政府債券上漲則顯示，成長制約仍能吸引存續期間需求。6月11日美債因油價大幅下跌而克服這些外部壓力，卻未消除支撐實質期限殖利率偏高的買方競爭及財政不確定性。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

新興市場債券受惠於共同背景轉趨寬鬆。隨美債殖利率與油價下跌，EMB上漲0.9649%；美元也小幅走弱，並未收緊全球流動性。原油下跌降低了燃料依賴型經濟體的前瞻進口帳單，並延後市場預期的Fed升息，同時減輕基準利率與匯率壓力。改善幅度足以推高上市硬通貨債券指標，但本地主權債表現仍主要取決於政策信用及再融資結構。

印尼仍是最明顯的不利案例。指標10年期本地債券殖利率上升10個基點至7.45%，接近2022年以來最高點；印尼盾下跌0.2%，年內跌幅仍約7%。外國投資人已從債券淨撤資$5.97億，並從股票淨撤資$39億。印尼央行曾表示，為支撐匯率將容許殖利率上升，分析師則認為還有25至50個基點的緊縮空間。因此，即使全球環境改善，匯率防衛仍在推高主權債的國內融資成本。

Danantara第1次發行的美元債顯示，只要付出代價，境外融資管道仍然可用。印尼主權財富基金透過規模相等的5年期及10年期券，籌得$15億。5年期債券殖利率為5.35%，2個券次的最終定價均較初始指引收緊約35個基點。最終訂單簿覆蓋率不到2倍，低於近期許多亞洲交易常見的3倍以上需求。這筆交易讓國家相關借款人取得市場融資，但超額認購幅度有限，加上近期印尼美元債利差走寬，使其與主權本身仍有區隔。

Danantara順利執行發行案與本地市場拋售之間的反差，凸顯印尼的政策問題。美元投資人接受一套界定清楚的債券與到期結構，本地債券卻仍須消化匯率防衛、石油進口成本及對干預式政策的疑慮。該基金也考慮發行票息低於市場水準的本幣債券，此提案可能考驗國內投資人支持策略性國家融資的意願。因此，成功進行境外發債雖緩解1項融資需求，卻未解決印尼盾殖利率曲線中包含的信心溢價。

辛巴威仍屬債務重整案例，而非市場融資故事。政府正洽談1筆$1.5億的African Development Bank貸款，作為更廣泛籌集$25億、用於清償積欠款項計畫的1部分。該國自1999年以來一直被排除於國際資本市場之外，目前尋求重整約$230億債務。IMF工作人員監督計畫及先前AfDB提供的$400萬補助，改善了制度性路徑，但過橋融資仍須轉化為債權關係正常化，傳統主權債發行才可能成為現實。

祕魯提供較穩定的政策對照案例。隨Lima通膨率由4.01%降至3.91%，央行連續第9個月將基準利率維持在4.25%。能源、天然氣及食品衝擊使通膨仍高於1%-3%的目標區間，但自10月以來首次出現月度降溫，降低了立即升息的理由。總統大選開票差距極微，使政治風險仍然存在，但利率決策顯示，具公信力的制度可以等待暫時性供給壓力消退，而非自動緊縮。

因此，新興市場收盤呈現的是圍繞持續國別分化的舒緩行情。油價下跌與美元走弱改善了共同融資環境，Danantara也展現取得外部資本的能力。印尼本地殖利率曲線仍要求政策與信心溢價，辛巴威仍依賴清償積欠款項，而祕魯只有在通膨開始緩和後才能維持利率不變。主權、準主權及企業融資管道對同一全球走勢的反應各異，因其匯率曝險、制度公信力及再融資選項不可互換。

## 跨資產

油價為美債漲勢提供最清楚的跨資產驗證。美國總統取消原定打擊行動，並表示可能宣布與伊朗達成協議後，WTI期貨下跌2.5769%至$87.71，Brent下跌2.9216%至$90.38。2項基準價格仍足以維持市場對上游通膨的擔憂，但單日跌幅已消除部分戰爭溢價。原油與損益兩平通膨率同步下降，使市場走勢與局勢降溫相符，而非一般性的需求衝擊。

股市強化了這項解讀。S&P 500上漲1.7519%，Nasdaq 100上漲3.2908%，半導體指數大漲7.9055%。VIX下跌12.5112%至19.44。若成長恐慌嚴重到足以推動美債殖利率下跌10個基點，通常很難與科技股如此強勁及波動率崩跌同時出現。相反地，能源風險降低改善了前瞻通膨與政策組合，同時維持名目成長及對長存續期間獲利的偏好。

美元與黃金也未呈現傳統避險格局。美元指數下跌0.09%至99.86，黃金期貨下跌0.4357%至$4,090.30。2項資產皆未顯示市場因地緣政治即將升級而尋求保護。美元小幅下跌，有助新興市場債券參與漲勢；黃金走弱則與戰爭保險需求下降及風險偏好轉強一致。這些走勢顯示，實質殖利率舒緩比爭相尋求安全資產更能清楚解釋美債上漲。

跨資產走勢仍留下一項未決問題。油價下跌源於市場相信談判取得進展，但談判立場在衝突期間曾一再轉變；生產者物價漲勢廣泛也顯示，先前能源壓力已進入部分供應鏈。股市對有利結果的定價，比單靠通膨數據所能支持的程度更為積極，而美債利率水準仍包含限制性的實質利率格局。這種一致走勢在單一收盤十分強勁，但能否持續，取決於局勢實際降溫，以及家庭與企業通膨預期持續回落。

## 市場前瞻意涵

6月12日10:00 ET公布的密西根大學 6月初值調查，接著是6月15日09:15公布的美國工業生產、6月16日08:30公布的進出口價格及新屋開工，以及6月17日08:30公布的零售銷售；這些數據將在當日14:00 FOMC決策前，檢驗能源衝擊是否正在消退。可觀察的觸發條件是家庭通膨預期再度上升，或廣泛貿易價格壓力與生產及支出保持韌性同時出現；另一種情況則是預期轉弱及利率敏感型活動降溫。前者將挑戰6月11日損益兩平通膨率下降、升息延後的解讀，並在主席Kevin Warsh首次政策溝通前，對2至10年期殖利率構成上行壓力。後者將證實申領失業救濟人數與油價下跌，正取代PPI成為邊際政策依據，使Fed可將利率維持在3.50%-3.75%區間，而不暗示近期升息。

美國財政部在6月15-18日的現金行事曆，將檢驗面對集中交割與新一波供給時，標售是否仍能有序承接。3年期、10年期與30年期附息債券的$1,190億總額將於6月15日交割，當日也將標售$890億的13週期及$770億的26週期國庫券；6月16日將標售$650億的6週期國庫券，並增額發行$130億的20年期美債，隨後於6月18日增額發行$240億的5年期TIPS。可觀察的觸發條件包括投標倍數、終端投資人與交易商占比、20年期標售結果相對當時發行前交易水準的差距、短期融資利率，以及TIPS標售的實質殖利率需求。若終端投資人強力承接且融資穩定，將證實6月11日漲勢由總體因素帶動，並化解部分資產負債表疑慮。若交易商獲配偏高、存續期間需求疲弱或交割承壓，則供給與準備金稀缺將再次成為期限溢價的獨立壓力來源。

日本央行及澳洲央行於6月16日公布決策，接著印尼央行、瑞士央行、挪威央行、英格蘭銀行及菲律賓央行於6月18日公布決策；這些事件將檢驗ECB升息後，油價下跌是否導致政策分歧。可觀察的觸發條件包括BOJ是否如預期升息至接近1%、澳洲央行與歐洲央行維持利率不變的決策是否保留緊縮措辭，以及印尼或菲律賓是否為防衛貨幣、抵禦輸入性通膨而升息。若JGB與英國公債長端穩定、新興市場貨幣走強且政策指引降低鷹派程度，將挑戰海外殖利率曲線必須持續推高美債出清利率的觀點。若進一步升息、存續期間資產出現失序賣壓或再度進行匯率防衛，則將證實全球資本競爭，並重新開啟因6月11日油價下跌而暫時緩解的輸入性通膨管道。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為準。

overall_summary.headline: 局勢降溫降低油價風險，美債走揚，但通膨仍使政策維持限制性
overall_summary.session_narrative: 截至完整紐約收盤，美債各期限皆上漲，由中段領漲，殖利率曲線溫和牛市趨平。局勢降溫的消息降低原油的戰爭溢價，推低損益兩平通膨率，並將市場完全定價的25個基點升息延後至2027年初；實質殖利率下降則顯示舒緩行情更為廣泛。生產者物價全面強勁、實質殖利率偏高及信用融資管道具選擇性，限制了敘事轉向寬鬆的空間。
overall_summary.summary: 市場對美國與伊朗達成協議的信心壓低原油與通膨補償，使美債全面上漲，取代前一交易日的熊市趨陡。實質殖利率亦告下滑，但熾熱的生產者物價、仍高的實質利率及海外緊縮，令升息延後的重新定價仍有條件。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤後，主導風險已由通膨型趨陡轉為有條件的地緣政治舒緩。
section_summaries.next_check: 實際局勢降溫必須讓油價與通膨預期在不只1個交易日內持續回落。
section_summaries.summary: 局勢降溫的希望壓低原油，逆轉前一交易日的熊市趨陡；隨著升息時點後移，名目與實質殖利率同步下降。熾熱的生產者物價、海外緊縮及具選擇性的信用融資管道，仍使限制性政策格局懸而未決。

section_summaries.market_read: 這波漲勢重新定價了限制性政策的時點，卻未開啟可信的政策寬鬆路徑。
section_summaries.next_check: 即將公布的通膨、經濟活動及FOMC訊號，必須釐清PPI與申領失業救濟數據之間的矛盾。
section_summaries.section_title: 美債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 美債全面上漲，10年期券領漲，帶動殖利率曲線溫和牛市趨平，實質殖利率與損益兩平通膨率則等幅下跌。緊縮延後、標售有序及準備金充裕為存續期間提供支撐，但生產者物價壓力廣泛，意味下一步政策行動只是延後，而非下調。

section_summaries.market_read: 信用市場證實風險偏好廣泛，但融資管道與定價仍取決於發行人品質。
section_summaries.next_check: 新券訂單簿必須顯示，在缺少異常明確的融資計畫時，強勁需求能否延續。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 初級市場消化了銀行及AI相關的大額融資，Oracle既有債券也在其融資計畫範圍變得較明確後走強。CoreWeave以高成本取得跨幣別融資管道，但Hanuman一再延後發行，顯示美債殖利率下降仍無法克服治理薄弱及現金流界線不清的問題。

section_summaries.market_read: 海外政策與財政風險使全球期限溢價無法驗證低利率格局。
section_summaries.next_check: 6月各央行決策必須揭示油價下跌是否降低進一步緊縮的壓力。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 能源通膨擴散促使ECB緊縮，但由於此舉符合預期，且成長轉弱限制後續行動，歐洲存續期間資產仍上漲。挪威經濟活動轉弱與英國財政不確定性使全球存續期間受到方向相反的拉力，令長天期美債買盤仍面臨競爭。

section_summaries.market_read: 全球環境舒緩支撐硬通貨債券，但並未消除主權信用溢價。
section_summaries.next_check: 匯率走勢與政策決策必須顯示，新興市場的舒緩能否承受油價壓力再起。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 油價下跌、美元走弱及Fed緊縮延後，改善了新興市場的共同背景，但各國結果仍高度分化。印尼的匯率防衛即使有境外融資管道，仍推高本地融資成本；辛巴威仍須清償積欠款項，而祕魯通膨降溫則容許央行保持耐心。

section_summaries.market_read: 風險資產驗證了通膨與政策組合改善，而非資金防禦性湧入美債。
section_summaries.next_check: 油價與家庭通膨預期必須持續下降，跨資產的一致走勢才能維持。
section_summaries.summary: 原油、損益兩平通膨率與避險需求下降，股市則大漲、波動率回落，證實美債上漲反映局勢降溫，而非成長衝擊。由於先前能源壓力已進入生產成本，且談判仍可能逆轉，這種一致走勢仍有條件。

section_summaries.market_read: 這波漲勢仍取決於總體通膨降溫、標售承接能力及全球政策分歧。
section_summaries.next_check: 關注6月12–18日的事件序列，是否出現損益兩平通膨率、融資或海外存續期間方面的失守。
section_summaries.summary: 收盤走勢能否持久將取決於3項考驗：美國數據與FOMC、集中的美債供給，以及全球政策決策的協同結果。通膨訊號轉弱、終端投資人有序承接及海外指引降低鷹派程度，將支持舒緩行情；相反結果則會重燃政策、融資及期限溢價壓力。

watch_events.market_implication: 前一種組合將對2至10年期殖利率構成上行壓力；後1種則會驗證升息延後。
watch_events.market_trigger: 預期與貿易價格上升且需求仍具韌性，或利率敏感型活動轉弱。
watch_events.title: 美國數據與FOMC政策考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤FOMC決策前的通膨預期、貿易價格、經濟活動及支出。

watch_events.market_implication: 壓力將使供給與準備金稀缺重新成為期限溢價壓力的獨立來源。
watch_events.market_trigger: 交易商獲配偏高、20年期標售結果疲弱或融資吃緊，將顯示承接不佳。
watch_events.title: 美債交割與標售承接
watch_events.watch_line: 觀察交割期間的投標倍數、投標人結構、融資利率，以及20年期美債與TIPS需求。

watch_events.market_implication: 前者將減輕美債出清壓力；後者則會重新開啟輸入性通膨風險。
watch_events.market_trigger: 長端穩定且新興市場貨幣走強，或進一步升息並再度進行匯率防衛。
watch_events.title: 全球央行分歧考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤BOJ、澳洲央行及6月18日各項決策的利率、指引與長端穩定性。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The June 11 close overturned the prior session’s bear-steepening expression without settling the restrictive-policy debate beneath it. Treasuries rallied from the 2-year sector through the long bond, and the 10-year yield finished at 4.45%, down 10 basis points. That outcome marked a decisive change from June 10, when softer core consumer inflation had failed to pull yields lower. The new catalyst was geopolitical: renewed confidence that the US-Iran conflict could move toward an agreement drove crude lower and removed part of the energy premium embedded in nominal yields and the expected Federal Reserve path.

The strongest transmission ran from de-escalation headlines through oil, inflation compensation and rate expectations. Short-rate contracts shifted a fully priced quarter-point increase from December into Q1 of 2027. Real yields also fell, showing that the rally was broader than a mechanical drop in breakevens. Lower energy risk eased the prospective policy constraint, improved the growth-inflation mix and encouraged duration demand even as equities surged. This was therefore an oil-led repricing of the shock distribution, not a defensive flight from growth.

Domestic data supplied the main counterforce. May final-demand producer prices rose 1.1% from April, with energy lifting goods costs and underlying measures also exceeding expectations. Initial and continuing jobless claims moved higher, however, preventing the inflation surprise from becoming an unqualified tightening signal. The combination left the Fed with a difficult asymmetry: pipeline inflation remained too strong to validate easing, but softer labor indicators and a lower energy risk premium reduced the urgency of an increase.

Supply and global policy kept the rally from becoming a clean return to low-rate assumptions. The long-bond reopening was absorbed with improved cover and a large direct bid, yet the long end still lagged the belly slightly. The ECB raised rates because energy inflation was broadening, and officials left open another near-term increase. Credit markets reinforced the same conditional message: high-grade banks and large AI borrowers accessed substantial funding, but governance-impaired issuance failed even at a high yield. The governing interpretation changed from inflationary steepening to broad geopolitical relief, while elevated real yields, selective credit access and foreign tightening preserved a high hurdle for declaring the restrictive regime resolved.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

The Treasury rally was broad, with the belly outperforming at the margin. The 2-year yield fell 8 basis points to 4.05%, the 5-year dropped 9 to 4.18%, and the 10-year declined 10 to 4.45%. The 30-year yield ended 8 basis points lower at 4.95%. The move returned the long bond below 5% and reversed the entire June 10 selloff, but policy-sensitive yields remained well above the federal funds target range. The close repriced the timing of possible restraint rather than introducing an easing path.

Curve changes were controlled. The 2s10s slope narrowed 2 basis points to 40, while 2s30s held at 90 because the 2- and 30-year yields fell equally. The 2s5s10s butterfly was unchanged at -14, leaving the 5-year sector’s relative shape stable. The 2s10s30s butterfly declined 4 basis points to -10 as the 10-year led the rally. This mild bull flattening contrasts with the prior day’s bear steepening, yet it did not create a new belly dislocation or an isolated long-end break.

Real yields and inflation compensation divided the nominal move evenly at 10 years. The 10-year TIPS yield fell 5 basis points to 2.16%, and the 10-year breakeven declined 5 to 2.29%. 5-year real yields also fell 5 basis points to 1.78%; 7-year yields dropped 6 to 1.96%, and 20- and 30-year real yields declined 5 and 6 to 2.53% and 2.72%. Falling breakevens captured the oil channel, while the parallel retreat in real yields reflected a lower perceived need for near-term policy tightening and less compensation for an adverse growth-inflation mix.

The longer comparison keeps the relief in perspective. 2-year yields were unchanged over 1 week and remained 10 basis points higher over 1 month. 5-year yields were also flat on the week and 11 basis points higher on the month. The 10-year was 2 basis points lower over 1 week but still 3 higher over 1 month, whereas the 30-year was 2 lower on the week and 3 lower on the month. The accumulated repricing remained concentrated in the front and belly, where policy timing and the expected neutral rate carry the most weight.

May producer prices challenged the rally’s durability. Final demand increased 1.1% on the month and 6.5% from a year earlier, against a 0.7% monthly consensus estimate. Final demand excluding food, energy and trade services rose 0.8% monthly and 5.1% annually, also above the 0.5% monthly estimate. Goods prices jumped 2.8%, led by a 10.7% increase in energy, while services rose 0.3%. The breadth beyond headline energy prevented the data from being dismissed as a single-component shock.

Labor data pointed the other way. Initial claims rose by 4,000 to 229,000 in the week ended June 6, above the 220,000 estimate, and the 4-week average increased to 219,000. Continuing claims climbed by 24,000 to 1.795 million in the week ended May 30. The insured unemployment rate held at 1.2%, so the release did not signal a sudden labor rupture. It did show more workers entering and remaining on benefits, supplying a timely counterweight to the wholesale-inflation surprise.

Short-rate contracts resolved that conflict by delaying, rather than deleting, the tightening risk. Before the geopolitical headline, markets had fully priced a quarter-point increase by December. By the close, that timing had shifted into Q1 of 2027. The reaction aligned with the joint fall in real yields and breakevens: cheaper oil reduced the risk of persistent pass-through, and firmer claims lowered the cost of waiting. Producer-price breadth still left the June 16-17 FOMC meeting unable to endorse a simple all-clear on inflation.

The $22 billion 30-year reopening cleared at 5.020% with a 2.33 bid-to-cover ratio, compared with 2.30 at the prior, larger sale. Indirect bidders took 59.95%, direct bidders 25.31% and dealers 14.74%. The 5% coupon bond settles June 15 and matures in May 2056. The cover and bidder allocation define the demand signal; without a contemporaneous when-issued yield, no tail inference is possible. Improved cover, an unusually large direct share and a modest dealer residual showed end-user capacity, although the smaller offering reduced the balance-sheet burden relative to May.

Bill auctions showed similarly orderly but differentiated demand. Treasury accepted $70 billion of 4-week bills at a 3.595% high rate with 3.13 cover, up from 3.07 previously; indirect bidders took 70.59%. The $75.001 billion 8-week sale cleared at 3.610% with 2.94 cover, down from 3.20, and a 69.59% indirect share. Both settle June 16. The shorter sale improved on a reduced amount, whereas unchanged 8-week supply drew softer cover. Neither result coincided with broad pressure in very-short cash yields.

Coupon-equivalent bill yields ended at 3.67% for 4 weeks and 3.68% for 6 weeks, both unchanged. The 8-week yield rose 1 basis point to 3.69%, while 13-, 17- and 26-week yields each fell 1 basis point to 3.71%, 3.76% and 3.80%. The 52-week yield declined 5 basis points to 3.85%, matching the delayed-hike adjustment in short-rate contracts. The front end remained upward sloping, but the policy premium migrated away from the immediate horizon.

Reserve management reduced the risk that the next increase in bill supply would become an abrupt funding shock. The Fed kept planned Treasury-bill purchases near $10 billion for the monthly period ending July 13 and scheduled about $16.5 billion of reinvestment purchases. Bank reserves had risen by $65.8 billion to $3.111 trillion in the week through June 10. Those operations are aimed at maintaining ample reserves as Treasury eventually rebuilds its cash balance above $1 trillion, a process that would otherwise withdraw liquidity from the banking system.

The rates close consequently carried two messages at once. Geopolitical relief was powerful enough to overpower a hot PPI release and pull both real yields and inflation compensation lower. Auction participation and reserve-management purchases kept supply from interrupting the move. Yet elevated producer-price breadth, an upward-sloping bill curve and still-high real yields prevented the rally from becoming an easing narrative. The next policy step looked later, not necessarily lower.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Credit participated in the broad improvement, with listed total-return gauges gaining alongside Treasuries and equities. LQD rose 0.8506%, HYG added 0.5914% and JNK gained 0.5745%. The larger investment-grade advance carried a meaningful duration contribution from falling government yields, while the high-yield gains showed that the session did not depend on a deterioration in corporate risk appetite. Primary markets remained open across ratings, although issuer structure and funding clarity produced sharply different outcomes.

Citibank demonstrated strong high-grade absorption with a $6.25 billion 4-part sale spanning 2 to 6 years. Price talk on the longest tranche tightened to 67 basis points over Treasuries from roughly 95 initially. The operating-bank notes were expected to carry Aa3, A+ and AA- ratings from Moody’s, S&P and Fitch, and proceeds were designated for general corporate purposes. Citi had issued less than peers after capital-rule changes reduced its urgency, so the transaction added supply without looking like distressed balance-sheet repair.

The bank deal sat inside an active investment-grade calendar. The other 5 largest Wall Street banks had already sold more than $120 billion during the year, including record Q1 issuance above $65 billion. Citi was one of 3 borrowers that brought more than $10 billion of US high-grade supply during the session. Tightening from initial talk showed that investors could absorb the late entrant despite the macro volatility, and the short-to-intermediate maturity range avoided placing the full burden on long-duration portfolios.

Oracle supplied a more specific cash-credit response to funding transparency. Its 3.65% note due 2041 tightened 15 basis points to a 2-percentage-point spread by midday, and the 6.9% bond due 2052 narrowed 12 basis points to 2.09 percentage points. 5-year default protection also became cheaper. The improvement followed guidance that Oracle did not expect another calendar-year bond sale and would fund its current fiscal year with a planned $40 billion mix of debt and equity, including $20 billion of previously announced at-the-market stock issuance.

That relief did not remove the scale of Oracle’s capital needs. Fiscal-year capital expenditure reached $55.7 billion, above the company’s $50 billion projection, and net capital spending in the new year was expected near $70 billion. Customer prepayments and customer-supplied computing equipment can reduce Oracle’s immediate cash burden, but the company remains the largest nonfinancial issuer in the US high-grade index with about $117 billion of bonds. Credit investors rewarded a bounded financing plan even as the equity market questioned the returns on the spending itself.

CoreWeave extended AI funding into riskier and more global channels. The junk-rated cloud company sold €2 billion of 6-year euro notes at an 8.5% yield and $1.25 billion of 6-year dollar notes at 9.625%. Euro orders exceeded €7 billion and drew most major European high-yield buyers. Ratings of Ba3, B+ and BB- left the transaction far below hyperscaler quality, while projected investment near $35 billion and cash burn near $26 billion kept refinancing and execution risk high. The orderbook showed access; the yield preserved a large premium for leverage and uncertain profitability.

Hanuman Wind Power marked the boundary of that access. The Energy Absolute unit postponed its international junk-bond debut for a second time after extending order taking by a day. A mid-to-high-9% yield did not overcome concerns about the Thai parent’s governance, past fraud allegations and the intended transfer of some proceeds to the parent. Rising 15-year Treasury rates also weakened the relative valuation of the proposed 15-year non-call-5 structure. Investors accepted aggressive AI expansion at a price, but rejected a structure whose governance and cash-flow perimeter remained unclear.

Primary absorption was therefore strong without becoming indiscriminate. High-grade bank debt tightened materially from initial talk, Oracle’s outstanding bonds rallied on a capped funding schedule, and CoreWeave diversified across currencies with a heavily subscribed euro tranche. Hanuman’s repeated postponement showed that yield alone could not repair weak governance. Falling Treasury yields improved the mechanical funding backdrop, yet disclosure, security position, buyer-base breadth and confidence in future capital needs determined how much of that benefit reached each borrower.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

The ECB moved against the direction of the US geopolitical repricing. It raised the deposit rate by 25 basis points to 2.25%, its 1st increase in almost 3 years, because the energy shock was broadening into indirect costs and inflation expectations. President Christine Lagarde described a slowdown in activity, especially services, but judged the economy able to absorb tighter policy. The decision made the euro area the first major economy to respond to the war-driven price shock with a hike.

European duration rallied despite the action. The 10-year yield fell 3 basis points to 3.05% after the announcement, and the euro held near $1.1538. That reaction suggests the hike was well anticipated and that the growth cost limited the market’s willingness to extrapolate an unlimited cycle. The ECB’s projections still kept inflation above target into H1 of 2027, leaving officials concerned that a peace agreement alone might not reverse second-round effects already moving through wages, services and expectations.

Follow-up risk remained meaningful. Officials did not exclude another increase as soon as July, although September remained the more common expectation, and investors priced 2 additional moves beyond the new 2.25% rate. The IMF assumed 50 basis points of cumulative tightening during 2026 and lifted its euro-area inflation forecast to 2.8% from 2.4%. It also reduced the growth outlook to 0.9% from 1.1%, defining the central bank’s constraint: broader inflation argues for restraint, but weaker demand can eventually reduce how much restraint is necessary.

Norway showed why the same energy shock need not produce synchronized action. A Norges Bank survey projected output growth of 0.2% in Q2 and 0.3% in the third, below the prior 0.4% Q2 view. Recruitment difficulty and capacity constraints eased, reducing pressure for another June hike despite a recent acceleration in core inflation. Wage-growth expectations rose to 4.5%, and the oil windfall supported the krone, so the case for holding rested on softer activity and a stronger currency rather than an absence of price pressure.

UK fiscal uncertainty added a separate long-duration premium ahead of the June 18 Makerfield by-election and Bank of England decision. Conventional gilts total about £2.1 trillion, while linkers and bills take public debt securities to roughly £2.9 trillion. National debt is projected at 105.4% of GDP in 2027, up from 98.8% in 2023. Investors remained wary of the long end because a possible Labour leadership challenge could alter borrowing plans, and the April budget deficit had already reached a 6-year high for that month.

These developments pulled Treasury duration in opposing directions. Expected ECB follow-through and unsettled UK fiscal policy raise the return available on competing sovereign curves and can make global buyers demand more from long US bonds. Softer Norwegian activity and the rally in European government debt show that growth constraints still attract duration. June 11’s US rally overcame those external pressures because oil fell sharply, but it did not eliminate the buyer-base competition or fiscal uncertainty that supports elevated real term yields.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt benefited from the softer common backdrop. EMB gained 0.9649% as Treasury yields and oil fell, and the dollar weakened slightly rather than tightening global liquidity. Lower crude reduced the prospective import bill for fuel-dependent economies and delayed the expected Fed hike, easing both base-rate and currency pressure. The improvement was broad enough to lift the listed hard-currency gauge, but local sovereign performance remained dominated by policy credibility and refinancing structure.

Indonesia remained the clearest adverse case. The benchmark 10-year local yield rose 10 basis points to 7.45%, near its highest since 2022, while the rupiah fell 0.2% and remained about 7% weaker for the year. Foreign investors had withdrawn a net $597 million from bonds and $3.9 billion from equities. Bank Indonesia had signaled that it would tolerate higher yields to support the currency, and analysts saw scope for another 25 to 50 basis points of tightening. Currency defense was therefore raising the sovereign’s domestic funding cost even as global conditions improved.

Danantara’s debut dollar bond showed that external access was available at a price. Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund raised $1.5 billion through equal 5- and 10-year tranches. The 5-year notes yielded 5.35%, and both tranches priced about 35 basis points tighter than initial talk. Final books were less than twice covered, below the three-times-or-better demand common in many recent Asian deals. The transaction gave a state-linked borrower market access, but the modest oversubscription and recent widening in Indonesian dollar spreads preserved differentiation from the sovereign itself.

The contrast between Danantara’s execution and the local selloff captures Indonesia’s policy problem. Dollar investors accepted a defined security and maturity package, whereas local bonds still had to absorb currency defense, oil-import costs and concerns about interventionist policy. The fund was also considering local-currency bonds with below-market coupons, a proposal that could test domestic investors’ willingness to support strategic state financing. Successful offshore issuance therefore eased 1 funding need without resolving the confidence premium embedded in the rupiah curve.

Zimbabwe remained a debt-restructuring case rather than a market-access story. The government was discussing a $150 million African Development Bank loan as part of a broader effort to raise $2.5 billion for arrears clearance. It has been excluded from international capital markets since 1999 and is seeking to restructure about $23 billion of debt. An IMF staff-monitored program and an earlier $4 million AfDB grant improve the institutional path, but bridge financing must still convert into creditor normalization before conventional sovereign issuance becomes realistic.

Peru provided a more stable policy counterexample. The central bank held its benchmark rate at 4.25% for a 9th month as Lima inflation eased to 3.91% from 4.01%. Inflation remained above the 1%-3% target range after energy, gas and food shocks, yet the 1st monthly moderation since October reduced the case for an immediate increase. A razor-thin presidential vote count kept political risk active, but the rate decision showed that credible institutions could wait for temporary supply pressure to dissipate rather than tighten automatically.

The EM close was thus a relief rally around persistent country dispersion. Falling oil and a softer dollar improved the shared financing environment, and Danantara demonstrated access to external capital. Indonesia’s local curve still demanded a policy and confidence premium, Zimbabwe remained dependent on arrears clearance, and Peru could hold only because inflation had begun to ease. Sovereign, quasi-sovereign and corporate channels responded differently to the same global move because their currency exposure, institutional credibility and refinancing options were not interchangeable.

## Cross asset

Oil supplied the clearest cross-asset confirmation of the Treasury rally. WTI futures fell 2.5769% to $87.71 and Brent dropped 2.9216% to $90.38 after the US president canceled planned strikes and said an agreement with Iran could be announced. Both benchmarks remained high enough to sustain concern about pipeline inflation, but the daily retreat removed part of the war premium. The simultaneous declines in crude and breakevens aligned the market move with de-escalation rather than a generic demand shock.

Equities reinforced that interpretation. The S&P 500 rose 1.7519%, the Nasdaq 100 gained 3.2908% and the semiconductor index jumped 7.9055%. The VIX fell 12.5112% to 19.44. A growth scare severe enough to generate a 10-basis-point Treasury rally would normally struggle to coexist with such strong technology performance and collapsing volatility. Instead, lower energy risk improved the prospective inflation-policy mix while preserving nominal growth and appetite for long-duration earnings.

The dollar and gold also resisted a conventional haven configuration. The dollar index slipped 0.09% to 99.86, and gold futures fell 0.4357% to $4,090.30. Neither asset showed demand for protection against an imminent geopolitical escalation. The modest dollar decline helped emerging-market debt participate in the rally, while gold’s weakness matched lower war insurance and firmer risk appetite. These moves left real-yield relief, not a scramble for safety, as the cleaner explanation for the Treasury advance.

Cross assets nevertheless kept one question open. Oil fell on confidence in negotiations that had repeatedly shifted during the conflict, and producer-price breadth showed that earlier energy pressure had already entered parts of the supply chain. Equities priced a favorable resolution more aggressively than the inflation data alone would justify, while Treasury levels still embedded a restrictive real-rate regime. The alignment was powerful for one close, but its durability depends on realized de-escalation and a continued retreat in household and business inflation expectations.

## Forward Market Significance

The June University of Michigan preliminary survey on June 12 at 10:00 ET, followed by US industrial production on June 15 at 09:15, import and export prices plus housing starts on June 16 at 08:30, and retail sales on June 17 at 08:30, will test whether the energy shock is fading before the FOMC decision at 14:00 that day. The observable trigger is a renewed rise in household inflation expectations or broad trade-price pressure alongside resilient production and spending, versus softer expectations and weakening interest-sensitive activity. The former would challenge June 11’s lower-breakeven, delayed-hike interpretation and pressure 2- to 10-year yields into Chair Kevin Warsh’s first policy communication. The latter would confirm that claims and cheaper oil are overtaking PPI as the marginal policy input, allowing the Fed to hold its 3.50%-3.75% range without signaling a near-term increase.

Treasury’s June 15-18 cash calendar will test whether orderly auction absorption survives concentrated settlement and a renewed supply sequence. The $119 billion gross settlement of 3-, 10- and 30-year coupons arrives June 15, alongside $89 billion of 13-week and $77 billion of 26-week bill auctions; June 16 brings a $65 billion 6-week bill sale and a $13 billion 20-year reopening, followed by a $24 billion 5-year TIPS reopening on June 18. Observable triggers are cover, end-user and dealer shares, the 20-year stop against its then-current when-issued level, short-term funding rates and demand for real yield at the TIPS sale. Strong end-user absorption with stable funding would confirm that June 11’s rally was macro-led and resolve part of the balance-sheet concern. Heavy dealer awards, weak duration demand or settlement strain would reopen supply and reserve scarcity as independent pressures on term premium.

The Bank of Japan and Reserve Bank of Australia decisions on June 16, followed by Bank Indonesia, the Swiss National Bank, Norges Bank, the Bank of England and the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas on June 18, will test whether lower oil produces policy divergence after the ECB’s hike. The observable triggers are whether the BOJ delivers the expected increase toward 1%, whether the RBA and European hold decisions retain tightening language, and whether Indonesia or the Philippines raises rates to defend currencies against imported inflation. Stable JGB and gilt long ends, firmer EM currencies and less hawkish guidance would challenge the view that foreign curves must keep lifting the Treasury clearing rate. Additional hikes, disorderly duration selling or renewed currency defense would confirm global competition for capital and reopen the imported-inflation channel that June 11’s oil decline temporarily relieved.

section_summaries.market_read: The close reset the dominant risk from inflationary steepening to conditional geopolitical relief.
section_summaries.next_check: Realized de-escalation must keep oil and inflation expectations retreating beyond 1 session.
section_summaries.summary: De-escalation hopes pulled crude lower and reversed the prior session's bear steepening, drawing nominal and real yields down as rate-increase timing moved later. Hot producer prices, foreign tightening and selective credit access still left the restrictive-policy regime unresolved.

section_summaries.market_read: The rally repriced the timing of restraint without opening a credible path toward policy easing.
section_summaries.next_check: Upcoming inflation, activity and FOMC signals must resolve the conflict between PPI and claims.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Broad Treasury gains produced a mild bull flattening as the 10-year sector led, while real yields and breakevens fell in equal measure. Delayed tightening, orderly auctions and ample reserves supported duration, but broad producer-price pressure kept the next policy step later rather than lower.

section_summaries.market_read: Credit confirmed broad risk appetite, but issuer quality still determined access and pricing.
section_summaries.next_check: New-deal books must show whether strong demand survives without unusually clear funding plans.
section_summaries.summary: Primary markets absorbed large bank and AI-related borrowing, while existing Oracle debt improved after its funding plan became more bounded. CoreWeave secured costly cross-currency access, but Hanuman's repeated postponement showed that falling Treasury yields could not overcome weak governance and unclear cash-flow boundaries.

section_summaries.market_read: Foreign policy and fiscal risks kept global term premiums from validating a low-rate regime.
section_summaries.next_check: June central-bank decisions must reveal whether lower oil reduces further tightening pressure.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: The ECB tightened as energy inflation broadened, yet European duration rallied because the move was anticipated and weaker growth constrained follow-through. Softer Norwegian activity and UK fiscal uncertainty pulled global duration in opposite directions, preserving competition for long-end Treasury buyers.

section_summaries.market_read: Global relief supported hard-currency debt without erasing sovereign credibility premiums.
section_summaries.next_check: Currency moves and policy decisions must show whether EM relief survives renewed oil pressure.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: Lower oil, a softer dollar and delayed Fed tightening improved the common EM backdrop, but country outcomes remained sharply differentiated. Indonesia's currency defense raised local funding costs despite offshore access, Zimbabwe still required arrears clearance, and Peru's easing inflation allowed patience.

section_summaries.market_read: Risk assets validated an improved inflation-policy mix, not a defensive rush into Treasuries.
section_summaries.next_check: Oil and household inflation expectations must keep falling for the cross-asset alignment to hold.
section_summaries.summary: Crude, breakevens and haven demand fell as equities surged and volatility retreated, confirming that the Treasury rally reflected de-escalation rather than a growth shock. The alignment remained conditional because earlier energy pressure had entered producer costs and negotiations could still reverse.

section_summaries.market_read: The rally remains contingent on macro disinflation, auction capacity and global policy divergence.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch the June 12–18 sequence for failures in breakevens, funding or foreign duration.
section_summaries.summary: 3 tests will determine whether the close becomes durable: US data and the FOMC, concentrated Treasury supply, and coordinated global policy decisions. Softer inflation signals, orderly end-user absorption and less hawkish foreign guidance would support relief; opposite outcomes would revive policy, funding and term-premium pressure.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.16% (-5 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.29% (-5 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 40 bp (-2 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 90 bp (0 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Treasuries rallied as de-escalation cut oil risk, but inflation kept policy restrictive
overall_summary.session_narrative: At the completed NY close, Treasuries had rallied across maturities, with the belly leading a mild bull flattening. De-escalation headlines lowered crude's war premium, easing breakevens and delaying the fully priced quarter-point increase into early 2027; falling real yields showed broader relief. Strong producer-price breadth, elevated real yields and selective credit access limited any shift toward an easing narrative.
overall_summary.summary: Broad Treasury gains replaced the prior session's bear steepening as confidence in a US-Iran agreement pulled crude and inflation compensation lower. Real yields also declined, but hot producer prices, still-high real rates and foreign tightening kept the delayed-hike repricing conditional.

watch_events.market_implication: The first mix would pressure 2- to 10-year yields; the second would validate a delayed hike.
watch_events.market_trigger: Rising expectations and trade prices with resilient demand, or softer interest-sensitive activity.
watch_events.title: US data and FOMC policy test
watch_events.watch_line: Track inflation expectations, trade prices, activity and spending into the FOMC decision.

watch_events.market_implication: Strain would restore supply and reserve scarcity as independent sources of term-premium pressure.
watch_events.market_trigger: Heavy dealer awards, a weak 20-year stop or strained funding would signal poor absorption.
watch_events.title: Treasury settlement and auction absorption
watch_events.watch_line: Watch cover, bidder mix, funding rates and 20-year and TIPS demand through settlement.

watch_events.market_implication: The first would ease Treasury clearing pressure; the second would reopen imported inflation.
watch_events.market_trigger: Stable long ends and firmer EM currencies, or added hikes and renewed currency defense.
watch_events.title: Global central-bank divergence test
watch_events.watch_line: Track BOJ, RBA and June 18 decisions for rates, guidance and long-end stability.
